commands.review_summary:
The branch renders non-empty `CronJob.description` values as a Summary row in the Control UI cron job detail and adds focused cron view tests for description, omission, ordering, and escaping.

PR surface: Source +18, Tests +93. Total +111 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a feature PR rather than a bug report. Source inspection confirms current main has stored cron descriptions and no visible description row, while the PR adds that rendering path.
commands.review_followup: Contributor-owned real behavior proof is still missing, and the remaining code change is a small i18n repair that should be reviewed with that proof rather than sent to an automated repair lane.

target.title: feat: render cron job description as Summary in Control UI
target.body:
## Summary

Cron jobs that carry a `description` now render it as a Summary section in the Control UI's job detail. Operators can see what a job is for without opening its source.

## Why this matters

Filed in #13479: cron jobs already have an optional `description` field on the data model, but the Control UI's job detail only rendered the prompt or systemEvent payload. Operators with many cron jobs had to open each job's source to remember what it was for. Surfacing the existing field closes the gap without adding new data.

## Changes

- `ui/src/ui/views/cron.ts`: adds `renderJobSummary()` and threads it into both branches of `renderJobPayload` (systemEvent and the default prompt path). Gated on `job.description?.trim()` so jobs without a description render unchanged.
- `ui/src/ui/views/cron.test.ts`: covers the systemEvent-with-summary, systemEvent-without-summary, prompt-with-summary, and whitespace-only-description cases.

## Testing

Vitest covers the four rendering paths. Default behavior for jobs without a description is unchanged.

Fixes #13479

AI was used for assistance.
target.author: mvanhorn
